subject: Project Management
title: Ken Schwaber's Contribution
description: A 60-second lesson on Ken Schwaber's Contribution.

hook.text: Ken Schwaber took Sutherland's experiment and turned it into a repeatable, teachable framework.

buildup.text: At OOPSLA 1995, Schwaber presented a formal paper on Scrum to the software community.

discovery.text: He defined roles, events, and rules — giving Scrum the structure it needed to spread.

twist.text: Schwaber later co-founded the Scrum Alliance, then left to create Scrum.org over a philosophical split.

climax.text: Two organizations now certify Scrum practitioners — born from one disagreement about commercialization.

punchline.text: Scrum's co-creators agreed on everything except the business.

quiz.question: What did Ken Schwaber do for Scrum?
options: Formalized it into a teachable framework with defined rules
Created the first Scrum software tool
